zh.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Courtenay, Feudal Baron of Oakhampton是John de Courtenay Feudal Baron of Oakhampton的兒子，於1251年出生在英國。他和Eleanor Despencer至少有1個女兒。他去世於1292年享年41歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Courtenay, Feudal Baron of Oakhampton是John de Courtenay Feudal Baron of Oakhampton的儿子，于1251年出生在英国。他和Eleanor Despencer至少有1个女儿。他去世于1292年享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron ‘baron’ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barūn ‘imperial or church official’, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain ‘son of the baron’. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Boroń): from a pet form of the personal names Borzysław or Borzymir, formed with the element bor ‘to fight’ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-Comté.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (Baró): status name from baró ‘free man, baron’ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (Báró): status name from báró ‘baron’. In the form Baró, it could be from a pet form of the personal names Barabás (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of Barón ‘baron’ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Simon , now rare, but more common in the Middle Ages, when it gave rise to the surnames Simms and Simpson.

variants.name: Simon
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

nameMeanings.name: Courtenay,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Courtney  .

variants.name: Courtney
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, originally a Norman baronial name from any of various places in northern France called Courtenay, the original meaning of which is ‘domain of Curtius’. However, from an early period it was wrongly taken as a nickname derived from Old French court nez ‘short nose’. In the U.K. it is found chiefly as a girl's name.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Courtenay, Feudal Baron of Oakhampton was born in 1251, in England as the son of John de Courtenay Feudal Baron of Oakhampton. He had at least 1 daughter with Eleanor Despencer. He died in 1292, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Courtenay, Feudal Baron of Oakhampton est né en 1251, à Angleterre, fils de John de Courtenay Feudal Baron of Oakhampton. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Eleanor Despencer. Il est décédé en 1292, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Courtenay, Feudal Baron of Oakhampton wurde 1251 in England, als Sohn von John de Courtenay Feudal Baron of Oakhampton geboren. Er hatte mit Eleanor Despencer mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1292 im Alter von 41 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Courtenay, Feudal Baron of Oakhampton è nato il nel 1251 nel England come figlio di John de Courtenay Feudal Baron of Oakhampton. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Eleanor Despencer. Morì nel 1292, all'età di 41 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Courtenay, Feudal Baron of Oakhamptonは1251年にイギリスでJohn de Courtenay Feudal Baron of Oakhamptonの息子として生まれた。彼はEleanor Despencerとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1292年に41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Courtenay, Feudal Baron of Oakhampton(은)는 1251년 영국에서 John de Courtenay Feudal Baron of Oakhampton의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Eleanor Despencer(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1292년 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Courtenay, Feudal Baron of Oakhampton nasceu em 1251, em Inglaterra filho de John de Courtenay Feudal Baron of Oakhampton. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Eleanor Despencer. Ele faleceu em 1292, com 41 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Courtenay, Feudal Baron of Oakhampton родился в 1251 г. в местности Англия. Его отца звали John de Courtenay Feudal Baron of Oakhampton. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Eleanor Despencer. Он умер в 1292 г. в возрасте 41 год.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Hugh Courtenay, Feudal Baron of Oakhampton nació en 1251, en Inglaterra como hijo de John de Courtenay Feudal Baron of Oakhampton. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Eleanor Despencer. Murió en 1292, a la edad de 41 años.
